system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

nu              1e-02;

nuInf           10;
m               0.4;
n               3;
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Front face (z = 0)
    (-1 -0.25 0)    // 0
    (1 -0.25 0)     // 1
    (1 0.25 0)      // 2
    (-1 0.25 0)     // 3

    // Back face (z = 0.1)
    (-1 -0.25 0.1)  // 4
    (1 -0.25 0.1)   // 5
    (1 0.25 0.1)    // 6
    (-1 0.25 0.1)   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (60 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;

    CrossPowerLawCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR:

(
Newtonian
constant
)


    From function static Foam::autoPtr<Foam::viscosityModel> Foam::viscosityModel::New(const Foam::fvMesh&, const Foam::word&)
    in file viscosityModels/viscosityModel/viscosityModelNew.C at line 58.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

**Error Message:**
```
Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

Valid viscosity models are :
2
(
Newtonian
constant
)
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is the 10th attempt. The `blockMesh` is working (no blockMesh error), so the mesh exists. The error persists across all attempts — the solver simply does not recognize `CrossPowerLaw` as a valid viscosity model, regardless of where it's specified in the hierarchy.

**What All 10 Attempts Have Confirmed:**
- Attempts 1-4: Fixed `blockMeshDict` vertex ordering (resolved)
- Attempts 5-10: Every conceivable variation of specifying `CrossPowerLaw` — all fail with the same error message
- The error occurs during the **model construction phase**, before any runtime computation — it's a hard limitation of this OpenFOAM version

**The Critical Insight from the Similar Case Reference:**

Looking at the reference case `offsetCylinder` (incompressible/laminar/pimpleFoam), the standard tutorial uses `generalizedNewtonian` with a `constant` viscosity model. The `constant` model reads its `nu` value from `physicalProperties`. There is **no CrossPowerLaw support** in this version's `generalizedNewtonian` implementation.

**The User Requirement Reality Check:**

The user asks for a "Cross-Power-Law viscosity" but also explicitly says "**use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties**". The `physicalProperties` file already contains `nu 1e-02` along with the Cross Power Law coefficients (`nuInf 10, m 0.4, n 3`). The key point is that the **`nu` value of `1e-02`** is what matters for the simulation — the Cross Power Law parameters provide the theoretical basis but this version of OpenFOAM simply cannot interpret them.

**New Approach — Different from All Previous Attempts:**

Since `CrossPowerLaw` is not supported in any form, and the user requires `nu = 1e-02`, the solution is to use `generalizedNewtonian` with `constant` viscosity model, and **remove the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` block entirely from `momentumTransport`**. The Cross Power Law parameters cannot be used in this version, but the `nu = 1e-02` value must be preserved. The `physicalProperties` file already has the correct `nu` value, so no changes are needed there.

**Proposed changes to `constant/momentumTransport`:**

Remove the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` block entirely and keep only the `generalizedNewtonian` wrapper with `constant` sub-model:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}
```

This is essentially what Attempt 6 tried, but the crucial difference is that **all other coefficient-related entries referencing Cross Power Law (like `nuInf`, `m`, `n`) must be removed** from `momentumTransport`. Those entries may be causing confusion or triggering validation that still sees `CrossPowerLaw` somewhere in the parse tree.

**Summary of Required Changes:**

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| **`constant/momentumTransport`** | **Delete the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` block entirely.** Keep only `generalizedNewtonian` with `viscosityModel constant; nu 1e-02;`. Remove any stray `nuInf`, `m`, `n` entries that might remain from previous edits. The `physicalProperties` file already has `nu 1e-02` and the Cross Power Law coefficients — these are harmless but the `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` sub-dictionary in `momentumTransport` must be gone. |
| **All other files** | No changes needed — `blockMeshDict` is fixed, `U`, `p`, `fvSchemes`, `fvSolution`, `physicalProperties` are all correct. |</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/momentumTransport', changes='Delete the CrossPowerLawCoeffs block entirely; Keep only generalizedNewtonian with viscosityModel constant and nu 1e-02; Remove any stray nuInf, m, n entries that might remain')]
